user_query:
I’m preparing a small, reproducible analysis to surface counter-narratives about the 1905 Dockside events from oral histories and to coordinate next steps with my team. Using only the files in input/, please design and implement a simple, transparent text-analysis heuristic that compares mainstream_claims.json to oral_histories.jsonl to flag likely contradictions. Then produce the artifacts below for our local workspace.

Inputs (read only):
- input/mainstream_claims.json
- input/oral_histories.jsonl
- input/meeting_agenda.md
- input/colleagues.json

Deliverables (all required):
1) output/counter_narrative_findings.csv
   - One row per claim_id from input/mainstream_claims.json (i.e., C1, C2, C3 must all appear exactly once).
   - CSV header columns (in this order): claim_id, mainstream_claim, stance, evidence_excerpt, evidence_source, confidence, method_note.
   - stance ∈ {contradicts, supports, unclear} based on your heuristic.
   - evidence_excerpt must be a verbatim substring from the "text" of some line in input/oral_histories.jsonl (≤ 280 characters). evidence_source must be "<speaker> (<year>)" from that same record.
   - confidence is a numeric score in [0,1] reflecting your heuristic’s strength (choose any interpretable mapping, but be consistent across claims).
   - method_note: 1–2 short phrases explaining which keywords/negation/duration cues drove the stance.
   - The heuristic should be deterministic, documentable, and limited to tokenization/keyword/negation/duration cues (no external models or web calls). It’s fine if it’s simple as long as it’s consistent and uses the provided "keywords" from mainstream_claims.json.

2) output/method_summary.md
   - ≤ 200 words describing your approach (steps, cues used, limitations). Keep it reproducible and human-auditable.

3) output/email_draft.txt (draft an email to colleagues)
   - To: a comma-separated list of recipients assembled from input/colleagues.json in the form "Name <email>".
   - Subject: Dockside 1905 counter-narrative findings — preliminary
   - Body: 100–180 words that (a) summarizes counts of {contradicts, supports, unclear} computed from output/counter_narrative_findings.csv, (b) briefly explains the method at a high level, (c) points explicitly to the two file paths you created (the CSV and method_summary.md), and (d) invites feedback on next steps.

4) output/email_rewrite.txt (rewrite for tone, clarity, and brevity)
   - Keep the exact same To and Subject lines as the draft.
   - Body: ≤ 120 words. Use a collegial, non-confrontational tone that centers community voices and avoids sensational language. Preserve all factual counts and file paths from the draft while making the message clearer and tighter.

5) output/meeting_notes.md (prepare notes/action items for the working group)
   - Use input/meeting_agenda.md for the Date and agenda context and input/colleagues.json for attendee names.
   - Include sections: Date, Attendees, Decisions, Action Items.
   - Decisions: at least one decision referencing a refinement to your heuristic (e.g., cue adjustments).
   - Action Items: at least three items. Each item must (a) reference a claim_id (C1/C2/C3), (b) assign an owner by name from colleagues.json, and (c) include a due date in ISO YYYY-MM-DD within 14 days of the Date in input/meeting_agenda.md.

Constraints:
- Do not use the internet or external APIs; keep everything local and deterministic.
- All paths above are relative to the project root. Create the output/ directory if needed.
- Ensure internal consistency: email counts must be derived from your CSV; evidence_excerpt must be a substring of oral_histories.jsonl; meeting attendees must match colleagues.json.

If anything seems ambiguous, document your assumption in method_summary.md rather than adding new inputs. Thank you! All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
